Small Area Estimation : Area Level Model

Paisan Leewattana

2026-03-18

Small Area Estimation : Area Level Model EBLUP Fay-Harriot Model Estimate : Amphor/District Level
This presentation uses data from district-level surveys, combined with Auxiliary Data from Administrative data and Remote sensing data. 
1. Direct estimates (\(\hat{y}_{i}^{dir}\)) : survey district level.
\(\hat{y}_i^{dir} = y_i + e_i\) , \(e_i\) = Sampling error \(e_i \sim N(0, \sigma_e^2)\)
2. Auxiliary Data (\(x_d\)) : Administrative data , Remote Sensing Data Geospatial data …etc.
3. Select X variable dont multicolinearlity : drop Variable VIF > 5
4. EBLUP Model \(y_{i}^{\prime}=x_{i}^{\prime}\beta+u_{i}\) , \(u_d\) Random area effect \(u_i \sim N(0, \sigma_u^2)\)
5. EBLUP FH Model  Yi = xiB + ui + ei 
6. Shrinkage Factor (Weighting \(\gamma_i\)) \(\hat{\theta}_i^{EB} = \gamma_i \hat{y}_i^{dir } + (1 - \gamma_i) \mathbf{x}_i^T \hat{\beta}\) , \(\gamma_i = \frac{\sigma_u^2}{\sigma_u^2 + D_i}\)
7. Evaluation

 **Direct Estimates : Survey VS Small Area Estimates (SAE): Fay-Harriot Model**

1.Import Data Frame

Load Data Frame

Install Package and Library

Load data frame SES survey and Create Direct Estimate and weight and Sample size(n)

df <- read_excel("ses2023.xlsx")
names(df) <- trimws(names(df))
df$adm2_pcode <- paste0(df$CWT, df$AMP)
df$adm2_pcode <- sprintf("TH%02d%02d",
                         as.numeric(df$CWT),
                         as.numeric(df$AMP))

# Y = consumer expenditure mean (persons/baht/month)

df <- df %>% rename(y =A11) # A11=consumer expenditure 
df <- df %>% rename(wgt = A52) # weight household
design <- svydesign(ids = ~1,  weights = ~wgt,  data = df )
direct <- svyby(~y, ~adm2_pcode,  design, svymean,   na.rm=TRUE ) 
n_table <- df %>% group_by(adm2_pcode) %>% summarise(n = n()) # n Sample size
direct <- merge(direct, n_table, by = "adm2_pcode")
head(direct,5)

Sampling Variance & GVF

Variance in Direct Estimate = Var(zji) \(\hat{\theta}\) = Y/N N=wji \(z_{ij} = \frac{1}{\hat{N}} \left(w_{ij}y_{ij} - w_{ij}\hat{\theta}\right)\) Smoothing Variance when low n and variance don’t stable \(CV\) (Coefficient of Variation) is \(\frac{SE}{\text{Mean}}\) ***GVF model ***\[\log(\sigma^2) = \alpha + \beta \log(n) + \epsilon\] \({\sigma}^2_{\text{smooth}} = \exp(\text{log(var)_pred} + 0.5 \cdot \hat{\sigma}^2)\)

\(CV = \frac{\sqrt{\text{Var}}}{\hat{y}} \implies CV^2 = \frac{\text{Var}}{\hat{y}^2}\)\(\hat{\sigma}^2\) is Residual Standard Error Square from GVF_Model

direct$vardir <- direct$se^2  #  se Taylor Series Linearization Variance Direct 
direct$cv <- direct$se/direct$y  # CV Direct
direct$cv2 <- (direct$se/direct$y)^2

gvf_model <- lm(log(vardir) ~ log(n), data = direct)  # log(Var) = B1log(n)+e

direct$vardir_pred <- predict(gvf_model, newdata = direct) 
sig2 <- summary(gvf_model)$sigma^2  # var(ei) of GVF  

direct$vardir_smooth <- exp(direct$vardir_pred + (sig2/2))

head(direct,5)

note: adjust design effect (DEFF) if DEFF ≈ 1.5–2.0  DEFF <- 1.8 direct \(vardir_adj <- direct\) vardir * DEFF
check n<5 variance not stable
\(Var(y) \approx [E(y)]^2 \cdot CV^2\) for y nomal distribution \(Var(\log y) \approx \frac{Var(y)}{[E(y)]^2}\) for y is high Outliers

Diagnostics Plot

Plot log(n) VS log(\(var\)) large n but var low Smoothing

2.Import Auxiliary Variables

Import Auxiliary Data

Y = consumption expenditure per person
X1 = Dependency
X2 = nighttime light (NTL)
X3 = Elevation
X4 = Slope
X5 = NDVI

Xi = …

Multicollinearity

Check Multi-Collinearity
- plot Correlation Matrix r>0.8 high drop variable

## 
## Attaching package: 'reshape2'
## The following object is masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     smiths

Variance Inflation Factor(VIF)

VIF for multicolinearity VIF = 1: No correlation at all V1 < VIF < 5: Some correlation, but acceptable (Normal level) 5 < VIF < 10: High correlation beginning (Caution advised) VIF > 10: Serious problem >>Edit by cut or composite variable or PCA Number of variable <= number area / 10

##       ntl_mean     dependency       poptotal elevation_mean     slope_mean 
##       2.174490       1.234912       1.108022       2.537025       3.624915 
##      ndvi_mean 
##       3.483196

3.EBLUP FH Model

mdata <- as.data.frame(mdata)

model_reml <- fh(
  fixed = y ~ dependency  + log_ntl +
    elevation_mean + slope_mean + ndvi_mean,
  vardir = "vardir_smooth", #  varinace direct estimate
  combined_data = mdata,
  domains = "adm2_pcode",
  method = "reml",        #  REML
  MSE = TRUE ,            #  Mean Squared Error
  transformation = "no", #
  mse_type= "analytical" )
## Please note that the model selection criteria are only computed based on the in-sample domains.

Coefficient Variable

## Call:
##  fh(fixed = y ~ dependency + log_ntl + elevation_mean + slope_mean + 
##     ndvi_mean, vardir = "vardir_smooth", combined_data = mdata, 
##     domains = "adm2_pcode", method = "reml", transformation = "no", 
##     MSE = TRUE, mse_type = "analytical")
## 
## Out-of-sample domains:  190 
## In-sample domains:  738 
## 
## Variance and MSE estimation:
## Variance estimation method:  reml 
## Estimated variance component(s):  2724649 
## MSE method:  prasad-rao 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                coefficients  std.error t.value   p.value    
## (Intercept)      6325.43232  919.29260  6.8808 5.953e-12 ***
## dependency        -68.07779   15.44102 -4.4089 1.039e-05 ***
## log_ntl          2546.96138  104.47632 24.3784 < 2.2e-16 ***
## elevation_mean     -1.70761    0.58052 -2.9415  0.003266 ** 
## slope_mean        115.38105   35.13307  3.2841  0.001023 ** 
## ndvi_mean        2605.78486  903.34312  2.8846  0.003919 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Explanatory measures:
##    loglike      AIC      BIC      KIC     AdjR2     FH_R2
## 1 -6589.59 13193.18 13225.41 13200.18 0.5748965 0.6184989
## 
## Residual diagnostics:
##                        Skewness Kurtosis Shapiro_W    Shapiro_p
## Standardized_Residuals 1.505889 9.785618 0.8950025 4.989063e-22
## Random_effects         1.265849 8.227290 0.9228461 5.538345e-19
## 
## Transformation: No transformation

Export FH Estimate

Random Effect (u_i) \[std.res_i = \frac{\hat{u}_i}{\sqrt{\widehat{Var}(\hat{u}_i)}}\] for \(u_i \sim N(0, \sigma_u^2)\)

If Outliners -> Transformtion data (log or XGBoost)

compare_plot(model_reml) # in sample
## Please note that since all of the comparisons need a direct estimator, the plots are only created for in-sample domains.

Gamma

Shrinkage Factor (\(\gamma\)) \(\gamma_i = \frac{\sigma_u^2}{\sigma_u^2 + \psi_i}\)

\(\sigma_u^2\) = Variance of Random area effect \(\psi_i\) = Sampling Variance

#In sample
gamma_reml <- data.frame(
  Gamma   = model_reml$model$gamma$Gamma,
  Domain  = model_reml$model$gamma$Domain )
summary(gamma_reml)
##      Gamma           Domain         
##  Min.   :0.7137   Length:928        
##  1st Qu.:0.7966   Class :character  
##  Median :0.8321   Mode  :character  
##  Mean   :0.8280                     
##  3rd Qu.:0.8654                     
##  Max.   :0.9263                     
##  NA's   :190
#write_xlsx(gamma_reml , "gamma_reml.xlsx")

Random effect & Residual & Fitted value

# In-sample
ind_domains <- model_reml$ind$Domain[!is.na(model_reml$ind$Direct)]
diagnostics_reml <- data.frame(
  adm2_pcode         = ind_domains,
  Random_Effect      = model_reml$model$random_effects,
  Real_Residual      = model_reml$model$real_residuals,
  Std_Real_Residual  = model_reml$model$std_real_residuals,
  Fitted_Value       = model_reml$model$fitted )
summary(diagnostics_reml)
##   adm2_pcode        Random_Effect     Real_Residual      Std_Real_Residual  
##  Length:738         Min.   :-6717.6   Min.   :-1687.96   Min.   :-2.040012  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: -904.8   1st Qu.: -179.00   1st Qu.:-0.245924  
##  Mode  :character   Median : -179.6   Median :  -36.64   Median :-0.049699  
##                     Mean   :    0.0   Mean   :    2.34   Mean   : 0.001052  
##                     3rd Qu.:  674.6   3rd Qu.:  124.48   3rd Qu.: 0.173901  
##                     Max.   : 9467.8   Max.   : 2469.80   Max.   : 2.833661  
##   Fitted_Value  
##  Min.   : 3643  
##  1st Qu.: 6284  
##  Median : 6802  
##  Mean   : 7651  
##  3rd Qu.: 8106  
##  Max.   :14942
#write_xlsx(diagnostics_reml , "diagnostics_reml.xlsx")

Direct estimate & FH estimate & MSE

# All Area  928 Amphor/row
FH_reml <- data.frame(
  adm2_pcode = model_reml$ind$Domain,
  Direct = model_reml$ind$Direct,  #in-sample
  EBLUP = model_reml$ind$FH,  # All 
  MSE_Direct = model_reml$MSE$Direct, #in-sample
  MSE_EBLUP = model_reml$MSE$FH)   # All
summary(FH_reml)
##   adm2_pcode            Direct          EBLUP         MSE_Direct     
##  Length:928         Min.   : 2517   Min.   : 3475   Min.   : 216721  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: 5733   1st Qu.: 5907   1st Qu.: 423700

##                     Mean   : 7653   Mean   : 7442   Mean   : 578268  
##                     3rd Qu.: 8838   3rd Qu.: 8153   3rd Qu.: 695542

##                     NA's   :190                     NA's   :190      
##    MSE_EBLUP

##  1st Qu.: 395721  
##  Median : 535242  
##  Mean   : 937736

4. Precision and Effective

##  3rd Qu.:555360  
##  Max.   :783748

Coefficient of Variation (CV)

\(CV = \frac{SE}{Mean}= MSE/Direct\)

CV Reduction = CV_Direct - CV_EBLUP

## CV reduction% 11.43853
## CV reduction 0.01269967
## CV Direct 0.1110254
## CV EBLUP 0.0983257
plot(FH_compare$CV_Direct, FH_compare$CV_EBLUP,
    xlab="CV Direct",
    ylab="CV EBLUP",
    main="CV Comparison",
    pch=19)
abline(0,1,col="blue",lwd=2)

Mean Square Error (MSE)

## MSE Direct 578267.8
## MSE EBLUP 470092.9

Residual

  • Random Effect (\(u_i\)): \(u_i = \text{Direct}_i - \text{EBLUP}_i\)

  • Standardized Residual: is Residual for Normality check

hist(model_reml$model$real_residuals,
     main = "Residual Distribution",
     xlab = "Residuals",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "black",
     breaks = 30)

qqnorm(model_reml$model$real_residuals)
qqline(model_reml$model$real_residuals)

Gamma & Shrinkage

Shrinkage size = EBLUP−Direct Gamma \(\gamma_i\) (Shrinkage Factor)

summary(model_reml$model$gamma$Gamma)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##  0.7137  0.7966  0.8321  0.8280  0.8654  0.9263     190
model_reml$model$variance # estimated random effect variance
## [1] 2724649
hist(model_reml$model$gamma$Gamma,
     main="Distribution of Shrinkage Factor",
     xlab="Gamma")

cat("Random effect",mean(model_reml$model$random_effects))
## Random effect -3.798445e-10
cat("Variance Model",mean(model_reml$model$variance))
## Variance Model 2724649
plot(gamma_reml$Gamma,
    ylab="Shrinkage factor (gamma)",
    xlab="Area",
    main="Shrinkage Factor by Area",
    pch=19)
abline(h = mean(gamma_reml$Gamma, na.rm = TRUE), col = "red", lwd = 2)

plot(model_reml$ind$Direct,
     model_reml$model$gamma$Gamma,
     xlab="Direct Estimate",
     ylab="Shrinkage Factor (gamma)",
     main="Shrinkage vs Direct Estimate",
     pch=19)
abline(h = mean(gamma_reml$Gamma, na.rm = TRUE), col = "red", lwd = 2)
